body.title: [sprint] Empty/blank dispatch response triggers tier fall-through (worker/designer fix)
body.intention: I intend that an empty model response is treated as a failure and falls through, not returned as a valid answer.
body.description:
**Problem:** empirically, worker/designer route to google_free/gemini-2.5-flash which returns EMPTY text (proven this session) while ollama_cloud returns real text. Empty isn't a hard error, so the cascade returns the blank as if valid.

**Shape:** Treat an empty/blank response as a fall-through trigger (continue to the next tier candidate, e.g. ollama_cloud). Root-cause the gemini-2.5-flash empty (likely a wrong/renamed model id vs the google_free available set) and fix or deprioritize it.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/rules_engine.py or device.py (empty-response -> fall-through), GoogleSource / models_registry (gemini model id).
**Design rules:** fall-through extends T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier's cascade; honest (empty != success).
**Scope boundary:** IN — empty-response fall-through + gemini-2.5-flash root-cause. OUT — hard-error fall-through (done in T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier).
**Test plan:** a worker-tier dispatch returns NON-empty text (falls through the google_free-empty to ollama_cloud, or gemini fixed); an injected empty response from a candidate advances the cascade.
**Completion criteria:** worker-tier dispatch returns non-empty text (proof); an empty candidate response triggers fall-through rather than being returned.
